---
title: "Events"
subtitle: "Upcoming and past Thinking with Agents events"
toc: true
---

## Upcoming

*More webinars are in the works — check back soon.*

---

::: {.past-events-section}

## Past Events

### TwA Webinar: Agentic Tools for Teaching

**June 17, 2026 · Online (Teams)**

How faculty can use AI tools for course design, student feedback, and teaching students to use AI effectively.

| Resource | Format |
|----------|--------|
| Emily's Slides: Student Data & Grading | [PDF](webinar-jun2026/twa-teaching-deck-emily.pdf) |
| Erkmen's Slides: Teaching Prep | [PDF](webinar-jun2026/twa-teaching-deck-erkmen.pdf) |
| Supply & Demand Explorer | [Live Demo](webinar-jun2026/supply-demand-explorer.html) |

#### What we covered

- **Emily — Student data & grading**: the FERPA decision (approved tool, de-identify first, or don't), what counts as identifiable student data (including the "mosaic effect"), and a live de-identification grading pipeline that never sees a student's name
- **Erkmen — Teaching prep**: turning a paper into a ready-to-teach lecture — notes, slides, discussion prompts, and exam questions — with a reusable lecture-builder skill, plus building a self-contained interactive tool that embeds in any LMS
- Q&A

---

### TwA Webinar: Agentic Tools for Research

**June 15, 2026 · Online (Teams)**

How AI tools are changing research workflows — and how to use them effectively.

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Deck 1: Foundations & Voice | [PDF](webinar-jun2026/twa-research-deck1-foundations-voice.pdf) |
| Deck 2: Website & Brainstorming | [PDF](webinar-jun2026/twa-research-deck2-website-brainstorming.pdf) |

**Sample materials:** [Project Context Kit](project-context-kit.qmd) — a worked HUB + session log and a prompt to build your own.

#### What we covered

- The AI landscape for research: what tools can and can't do
- Building a writing voice file so AI output sounds like you
- Live demos: redesigning a website and brainstorming a research idea with an agent
- Building verification habits for AI-generated content

---

### UVM Economics AI Bootcamp · Spring 2026

A two-session faculty bootcamp held at UVM, covering agentic AI tools for research and teaching.

---

### Session 1: Getting Started with AI Tools

**April 22, 2026 · 11:00–12:30 · Old Mill A500**

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Session 1: Agentic AI Bootcamp (Aslim & Beam) | [PDF](Session1_Slides/session_1_slides.pdf) |

#### What we covered

- What AI tools exist and the difference between chat-based and agentic tools
- Context windows, standing instructions, and planning — the key concepts
- What we've actually used these for (and what was fun but saved zero time)
- Voice files: making AI output sound like you
- Skills: reusable workflows you can share
- Live demos: website redesign and custom skill creation

---

### Session 2: Research Workflows, Teaching & Applications

**April 27, 2026 · 11:00–12:30 · Old Mill A500**

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Session 2: Research Workflows, Teaching & Applications (Aslim & Beam) | [PDF](Session2_Slides/session_2_slides.pdf) |

#### What we covered

- **Emily: Research workflows** — code auditing a replication package with [`/code-review`](skills-workflows.qmd#code-review), paper auditing with [`/econ-audit`](skills-workflows.qmd#econ-audit) and [`/review-paper`](skills-workflows.qmd#review-paper), running Lee bounds live
- **Erkmen: Teaching & applications** — building a lecture-builder skill with [`/academic-beamer-deck`](skills-workflows.qmd#academic-beamer-deck), stress-testing a research idea with [`/research-brainstorm`](skills-workflows.qmd#research-brainstorm), web scraping with [`/find-data`](skills-workflows.qmd#find-data)

:::
